frozen_components: teacher LLM (gpt-3.5-turbo, used only for data synthesis)
Python interpreter (used for data filtering and execution)
pre-trained reasoning scorer (used for step-wise beam search)
architecture.summary: PaD is a distillation pipeline that transfers reasoning ability from LLMs to small models (CodeT5, 60M–770M parameters) by synthesizing executable Python reasoning programs from teacher LLMs via in-context learning, filtering them with an external Python interpreter for correctness, fine-tuning the small model on the filtered program data, and enhancing generation via self-refinement (multi-task learning with injected error cases) and step-wise beam search (scoring candidate intermediate steps using a pre-trained semantic alignment function before auto-regressive continuation).
trained_components: small student model (CodeT5) fine-tuned on program data

biblio.title: PaD: Program-aided Distillation Can Teach Small Models Reasoning Better than Chain-of-thought Fine-tuning

claims.statement: smaller models using PaD can not only outperform certain LLMs (e.g., LLaMA-1 13B) but also achieve strong improvement over baselines with a significantly smaller scale of parameters and data
Comparing with stand fine-tune, PaD achieves average 40% increase
PaD achieves a 10% improvement while utilizing just 35% of the baseline model's data size

baseline.name: Fu et al. (2023) with 130K samples, 0.76B model

claims.statement: PaD accomplishes comparable performance utilizing merely 4.5% of the baseline model's data size
on symbolic reasoning tasks (Coin Flip and Last Letter Concatenation), PaD achieves 100% accuracy across all CodeT5 model sizes
PaD reaches nearly 49% of GPT-4's, 61% of GPT-3.5's, and 79% of LLaMA-2 70B's performance in mathematical reasoning
While improving the reasoning capability of smaller models, it leads to a decline in general abilities. significant drop in BBH alongside an increase in reasoning performance.

PaD effectively narrows the prediction space, focusing on specific central points. Reasoning programs in Python exhibit clearer and simpler syntax compared to natural language in CoT, reducing the complexity of the task by adhering to Python syntax instead of extensive sampling across the entire language representation space.
